description: Hierarchical YAML configuration stack for the dagstack ecosystem — env interpolation, layered merge, typed sections, hot-reload, secret masking. One contract across Python, TypeScript, Go.
rules: Install with: pip install dagstack-config (Python, pypi.org) / npm install @dagstack/config (TypeScript, npmjs.org) / go get go.dagstack.dev/config (Go vanity URL backed by github.com/dagstack/config-go).

Load configuration with Config.load(path) — it reads base file, discovers app-config.local.yaml and app-config.${DAGSTACK_ENV}.yaml siblings, merges all three layers (base → local → env), performs env-interpolation, returns Config object.

Config layering: base app-config.yaml is checked into repo; app-config.local.yaml is gitignored (developer overrides); app-config.${ENV}.yaml is environment-specific (production, staging). Merge is deep for maps, arrays are replaced atomically.
DAGSTACK_ENV is the canonical env variable for selecting environment layer — use this instead of APP_ENV / NODE_ENV / RAILS_ENV to avoid cross-framework collisions in multi-framework deployments.

Env interpolation: ${VAR} raises ConfigError(env_unresolved) if VAR not set; ${VAR:-default} falls back to literal default. Interpolated values are always strings; typed coercion happens at typed getter call.
Escape literal dollar sign in YAML values with $$ → $. Applies to any string value, including contexts without ${...}.

Access config values by dot-notation paths: config.get('database.host'), config.getInt('cache.ttl_min'). Array indexing: config.get('plugins[0]'), config.getList('plugins'). Casing per language: snake_case (Python), camelCase (TS), PascalCase (Go).

Use config.getSection(path, schema) for typed access — it validates against pydantic BaseModel (Python), zod schema (TypeScript), or struct tags (Go). On failure it raises ConfigError(validation_failed) pointing at the specific field.
Plugins read only their own section via config.getSection(plugin.kind + '.' + plugin.name, PluginConfigSchema). Reading sibling plugin sections is an anti-pattern.
Primitive getters: get (raw Value), getString, getInt, getNumber, getBool, getList, has. Without default arg, missing key raises ConfigError(missing). Type mismatch raises ConfigError(type_mismatch).

ConfigError has structural fields: path (dot-notation), reason (missing | type_mismatch | env_unresolved | validation_failed | parse_error | source_unavailable | reload_rejected), details (human message), source_id (optional).

Secrets are masked in diagnostic output by field name pattern: api_key, secret_key, access_token, password, client_secret — and any field ending in _secret, _token, _password, _key. Full list in config-spec/_meta/secret_patterns.yaml. This is Phase 1 auto-masking (ADR-0001) — independent of Phase 2 ${secret:...} references.

Subscribe to runtime config changes via on_section_change / onSectionChange / OnSectionChange (per language) — returns Subscription with unsubscribe, active, inactive_reason. Sources without watch register active=false reason subscription_without_watch.
Hot-reload is atomic at tree-level: if reload validation fails for any subscriber, entire reload is rolled back, no subscribers are notified. This prevents partial-application of invalid configs.

ConfigSource is the abstraction for non-file sources — Phase 2+ adapters for etcd, Consul, HTTP, SQL, Kubernetes ConfigMaps. SecretSource is the parallel abstraction for secret backends (env, Vault). Each source provides id, load(), optional watch(callback), close() contract.

Canonical JSON format (RFC 8785 subset) is used for deterministic serialization in cross-process transmission and hash computation. Sorted keys, no trailing whitespace, LF line endings, numbers without unnecessary decimals.

Use Config.loadFrom(sources[]) for explicit source layering — precedence equals argument order (first is lowest priority, last is highest). Replaces auto-discovery with explicit listing when needed. The list may mix ConfigSource and SecretSource freely; the loader dispatches by interface.
YAML wire-format is identical across languages — same file parses to same Config in Python / TypeScript / Go. JSON is acceptable as equivalent wire-format (YAML 1.2 is JSON superset).

Do not write secrets into app-config.yaml (which is committed to git) — use ${secret:env:VAR} or ${secret:vault:secret/path#field}. The Config stack resolves them at load time (eager) or first read (Python lazy default); raw values never reach git.

Use app-config.local.yaml for developer-specific overrides that should not be committed — it's in default .gitignore. For environment-specific public overrides use app-config.${DAGSTACK_ENV}.yaml (committed).

Phase 2 (ADR-0002) adds ${secret:<scheme>:<path>} references for centralised secret backends. Syntax: ${secret:env:VAR}, ${secret:vault:secret/dagstack/prod/db#password}, ${secret:vault:secret/path?version=3#field}, ${secret:env:VAR:-default}. Scheme grammar: [a-z][a-z0-9_]*. Auto-registered EnvSecretSource handles env scheme; ${secret:env:VAR} is identical to ${VAR}.

Reference grammar (ADR-0002 §1): ${secret:<scheme>:<path>[?<query>][#<field>][:-<default>]}. Escape `?` as `??`, `#` as `##`, `:-` as `::-` inside path. Percent-encode `&`, `=`, `}`, `#` in query values per RFC 3986. Order is fixed: path → ?query → #field → :-default → }.

SecretSource is the contract for secret backends — distinct from ConfigSource. Fields: scheme (lowercase identifier matching the reference token), id (URI-style like vault:https://vault.example.com, carried in diagnostics), resolve(path, ctx) → SecretValue, optional close(). Phase 2 ships EnvSecretSource (mandatory) and VaultSource (optional opt-in).

SecretRef is the opaque placeholder a file source emits for an unresolved ${secret:...} token — carries scheme, path, default?, origin_source. Lives in the merged tree until resolution (Python lazy: first get*; TS/Go eager: at loadFrom). snapshot() replaces every SecretRef with [MASKED] without resolving — pass include_secrets=True / WithIncludeSecrets() to resolve in audit mode.

Resolution timing per binding: Python lazy by default (Config.load_from(..., eager_secrets=True) opts in to eager); TypeScript eager by default (loadFrom is async, pays cost up-front); Go eager by default (LoadFrom returns *Config, error). For long-lived servers prefer eager — secret_unresolved surfaces at startup, not at first request.

Cache: a resolved SecretValue is cached for the Config lifetime, keyed by <scheme>:<full-path>. SecretValue.expires_at / expiresAt / ExpiresAt is honoured — entries with past expiry are treated as cache misses. config.refresh_secrets() / refreshSecrets() / RefreshSecrets() drops the cache and re-resolves on next access — the Phase 2 manual rotation hook (push-based rotation is Phase 3).

VaultSource (HashiCorp Vault KV v2, ADR-0002 §6) — opt-in extra: pip install 'dagstack-config[vault]' (hvac>=2.0,<3.0) / npm install node-vault (optional peer-dep) / go get go.dagstack.dev/config/vault (separate sub-module). Auth: TokenAuth (token), AppRoleAuth (role_id, secret_id), KubernetesAuth (role, jwt_path). Namespace via construction param. Token self-renewal is deferred to Phase 3 — use long-TTL+restart, AppRole, or k8s SA auth in Phase 2.

Vault path layout — user-visible path is what `vault kv get` accepts (secret/dagstack/prod/openai); first segment is the KV v2 mount, the rest is the logical key path. The adapter rewrites <mount>/data/<path> for the HTTP API. ?version=N pins a specific KV v2 version (cache key includes version). #field projects a sub-key from a JSON envelope; the loader caches by path-without-#field so #password and #username share one Vault read.

Three new ConfigErrorReason values in Phase 2: secret_unresolved (no source, key missing, version destroyed, #field absent — check YAML / backend spelling), secret_backend_unavailable (network / DNS / auth at connect time — check connectivity), secret_permission_denied (Vault 403 / AWS-SM AccessDenied / K8s RBAC — check backend policy). Distinct codes so operators dispatch programmatically.

Go stutter-avoidance for Phase 2: config.SecretSource (not stuttering because package is `config` and prefix is `Secret`), config.SecretRef, config.SecretValue, config.EnvSecretSource. Vault adapter is in a sub-package: vault.Source (not vault.VaultSource), vault.NewSource(...), vault.TokenAuth / AppRoleAuth / KubernetesAuth, vault.WithNamespace / WithTLSConfig functional options.

Schemes are operator-extensible strings, not a closed enum. Spec reserves env (Phase 2 mandatory), vault (Phase 2 optional), awssm / gcpsm / k8ssecret (Phase 3 reserved). To register two Vault clusters, override the scheme to vault-prod and vault-dr (subclass in Python/TS, wrap in Go). The reserved names live in config-spec/_meta/secret_schemes.yaml.

EnvSecretSource is auto-registered by the loader if you don't pass one — ${secret:env:VAR} works without ceremony. Construct one explicitly only when you need a custom getenv: EnvSecretSource(getenv=env_dict.get) in Python, new EnvSecretSource({lookup}) in TypeScript, &config.EnvSecretSource{Lookup: ...} in Go. The env scheme does NOT support ?query or #field — env values are opaque strings; use VaultSource for JSON-typed secrets.

The language-agnostic contract for Phase 2 is fixed in config-spec/adr/0002-secret-references-and-sources.md plus three _meta files: secret_schemes.yaml (scheme registry), secret_ref_grammar.yaml (EBNF + per-binding regex fragments), error_reasons.yaml (three new reasons). Phase 3 (push rotation, cloud secret managers, token renewal) is tracked as ADR-0003 candidate — no normative decisions yet.
